summary: Do not ship: the RED proof is sound, but Round 17’s normative `atomic.rs` keep-map omits load-bearing production APIs. Simpler alternative: leave `atomic.rs` intact except for widening `fsync_dir_blocking`; move only the absence-related APIs. Open question: none.

findings.title: The atomic keep-map deletes the existing commit pipeline
findings.body: The scope says only `write_atomic`, `rename_durable`, `fsync_dir{,_blocking}`, and `mkdir_p_durable` remain in `atomic.rs`. At `red.sha`, however, `pins.rs:369` calls `stage_blocking` and then `Staged::commit_blocking`, while `objects.rs:75` calls `unique_suffix`; `rename_durable_blocking` and `mkdir_p_durable_blocking` are also required internally. Following this normative text either fails compilation or forces an unspecified rewrite of the uncancellable pointer-commit and durable-directory machinery, risking cancellation and durability regressions unrelated to F-14.
findings.recommendation: Replace the exclusive keep-list with an explicit statement that all pre-existing atomic APIs—including `stage_blocking`, `Staged`, `unique_suffix`, `rename_durable_blocking`, and `mkdir_p_durable_blocking`—remain unchanged. Specify that only the absence-related symbols move and only `fsync_dir_blocking` visibility changes.

next_steps: Correct both exclusive `atomic.rs` keep-lists, including the matching text at lines 237–240.
Re-run the document-only plan review; the committed RED record does not need regeneration.
